candidates.why: Explicit separate operating D&A summary line above EBIT, but it is not labelled as COGS/cost of sales related and appears to cover total operating D&A rather than a COGS-only amount.
Component of the total D&A line; not specifically tied to COGS and should not be chosen over the summary if a summary exists.
Granular amortization component beneath the broader D&A section; not specifically tied to COGS.
Granular amortization component beneath the broader D&A section; not specifically tied to COGS.

variables.notes: A separate operating D&A section exists, so D&A is not obviously embedded in the selected COGS rows. However, the excerpt does not functionally split D&A between COGS and SG&A. Because the visible D&A rows are consolidated operating amounts rather than COGS-specific rows, adding any of them to COGS would be arbitrary and risks misallocation/double counting.

candidates.why: Explicit separate operating D&A summary line above EBIT, sitting alongside Employee benefits expenses and Other expenses rather than clearly inside them; however it appears to be total operating D&A, not SG&A-specific.
Component of the total D&A line; should not be selected when a broader summary exists, and it is not labelled as SG&A-specific.
Granular amortization component under the broader D&A section; not specifically tied to SG&A and could overlap the summary.
Granular amortization component under the broader D&A section; not specifically tied to SG&A and could overlap the summary.

variables.notes: The statement shows a separate D&A line above EBIT, so D&A is not clearly embedded in the selected XSGA component rows. But there is no SG&A-only D&A row, nor any label indicating allocation to SG&A versus COGS. The dedicated R&D expense row is below EBIT and there is no explicit 'Amortization in R&D' row to handle as a special case. Conservative treatment is to add nothing here unless a manual functional allocation is available.
